Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. IMPORTANT: Comments are subject to validation constraints enforced by the MCP server - maximum 65536 characters for the complete comment (including footer which is added automatically), 10 mentions (@username), and 50 links. Exceeding these limits will result in an immediate error with specific guidance. NOTE: By default, this tool requires discussions:write permission. If your GitHub App lacks Discussions permission, set 'discussions: false' in the workflow's safe-outputs.add-comment configuration to exclude this permission. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation. A footer (~200-500 characters) is automatically appended with workflow attribution, so leave adequate space. If these limits are exceeded, the tool call will fail with a detailed error message indicating which constraint was violated.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool auto-targets the issue, PR, or discussion that triggered this workflow. Auto-targeting only works for issue, pull_request, discussion, and comment event triggers — it does NOT work for schedule, workflow_dispatch, push, or workflow_run triggers. For those trigger types, always provide item_number explicitly, or the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"}},{"name":"add_labels","description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the issue or PR that triggered this workflow. Only works for issue or pull_request event triggers. For schedule, workflow_dispatch, or other triggers, item_number is required — omitting it will silently skip the label operation.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"}},{"name":"noop","description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"}},{"name":"missing_data","description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":[],"type":"object"}}]}}

2026-03-14T12:08:48.6782387Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-14T12:07:00.463822418Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57104,\"title\":\"Provisioning RegenSdkLocal script deletes tspconfig.yaml, losing emitter options\",\"body\":\"## Problem\\n\\nThe provisioning \\\\Invoke-SdkRegeneration.ps1\\\\ script deletes \\\\\\tspconfig.yaml\\\\ from the fetched spec directory (line 175) to avoid linter rules that require packages not in the local \\\\\\node_modules\\\\. It then manually reconstructs emitter options via \\\\--option\\\\ flags.\\n\\nThis is inconsistent with the mgmt \\\\Invoke-SdkRegeneration.ps1\\\\, which keeps \\\\\\tspconfig.yaml\\\\ in place and lets \\\\\\tsp compile\\\\ read options naturally. The mgmt script only removes \\\\package.json\\\\.\\n\\n## Impact\\n\\n- Options set in \\\\\\tspconfig.yaml\\\\ (e.g. \\\\\\u0007pi-version\\\\, \\\\\\namespace\\\\, etc.) were silently dropped until a manual extraction workaround was added.\\n- Any new options added to the spec\\u0026#39;s \\\\\\tspconfig.yaml\\\\ for the provisioning emitter need to be explicitly forwarded in the script — easy to miss.\\n\\n## Current Workaround\\n\\nThe script now reads all provisioning emitter options from \\\\\\tspconfig.yaml\\\\ before deleting it and forwards them as \\\\--option\\\\ flags. This works but is fragile.\\n\\n## Ideal Fix\\n\\nAlign with the mgmt script approach: keep \\\\\\tspconfig.yaml\\\\ but strip the \\\\linter\\\\ section (or the \\\\\\u001bxtends\\\\ within it) to avoid the linter dependency issue, rather than deleting the entire file.\\n\\n## Related Files\\n\\n- \\\\\\u001bng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/eng/scripts/Invoke-SdkRegeneration.ps1\\\\ (provisioning — deletes tspconfig)\\n- \\\\\\u001bng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/eng/scripts/Invoke-SdkRegeneration.ps1\\\\ (mgmt — keeps tspconfig)\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57104\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"ArcturusZhang\",\"id\":10554446,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/ArcturusZhang\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/10554446?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-03-14T12:04:29Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-14T12:04:29Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

Generator] FlattenPropertyVisitor incorrectly flattens discriminator base types with few properties\",\"body\":\"## Bug\\n\\nWhen a discriminator base model type has only a single non-discriminator property, the `FlattenPropertyVisitor` in the mgmt generator marks it as `internal` (flattening it). This prevents users from working with the base type in polymorphic hierarchies.\\n\\n### Example\\n\\nGiven a TypeSpec discriminated model:\\n\\n```tsp\\n@discriminator(\\\"kind\\\")\\nmodel BackupPolicy {\\n  kind: string;\\n  retentionDays?: int32;\\n}\\n\\nmodel PeriodicBackupPolicy extends BackupPolicy {\\n  kind: \\\"Periodic\\\";\\n  intervalInHours: int32;\\n}\\n```\\n\\nThe generated `BackupPolicy` class is emitted as `internal` because after the discriminator property `kind` is excluded, it has only one property (`retentionDays`), triggering the `FlattenPropertyVisitor`'s flattening logic.\\n\\n### Expected\\n\\nDiscriminator base types should remain `public` regardless of property count, since users need to reference them as the polymorphic base type.\\n\\n### Actual\\n\\n```csharp\\ninternal partial class BackupPolicy : ProvisionableConstruct  // should be public\\n```\\n\\n### Context\\n\\nThis was discovered during development of the Azure provisioning generator (`eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/`).\\n\\nRelated PR: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/56627\\r\\n\",\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"ArcturusZhang\",\"id\":10554446,\"node_id\":\"MDQ6VXNlcjEwNTU0NDQ2\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/10554446?v=4\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/ArcturusZhang\",\"gravatar_id\":\"\",\"type\":\"User\",\"site_admin\":false,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang\",\"events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/events{/privacy}\",\"following_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/following{/other_user}\",\"followers_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/followers\",\"gists_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/gists{/gist_id}\",\"organizations_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/orgs\",\"received_events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/received_events\",\"repos_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/repos\",\"starred_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/starred{/owner}{/repo}\",\"subscriptions_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/ArcturusZhang/subscriptions\"},\"labels\":[{\"id\":1178447860,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Client\",\"name\":\"Client\",\"color\":\"ffeb77\",\"description\":\"This 2026-03-14T12:08:48.6933700Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-14T12:08:28.560130284Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage\n\n**Summary:** The `Invoke-SdkRegeneration.ps1` script in `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning` deletes `tspconfig.yaml` from the fetched spec directory to bypass linter dependency issues, then manually reconstructs emitter options via `--option` flags. This is fragile and inconsistent with the mgmt script approach (which keeps `tspconfig.yaml` and only removes `package.json`).\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e📋 Issue Analysis\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n**Root Cause:**\nThe provisioning regen script removes the entire `tspconfig.yaml` to avoid linter rules that require packages not present in `node_modules`. As a result, any emitter options in `tspconfig.yaml` (e.g., `api-version`, `namespace`) are silently dropped unless the script explicitly re-forwards them as `--option` flags.\n\n**Impact:**\n- New emitter options added to a spec's `tspconfig.yaml` must be manually plumbed through the script — an easy-to-miss failure mode.\n- The current workaround (reading all provisioning emitter options before deletion) works but is brittle and adds maintenance burden.\n\n**Proposed Fix:**\nRather than deleting `tspconfig.yaml`, selectively strip the `linter` section (or the `$extends` within it) so linter-only dependencies are removed while all emitter options are preserved. This aligns with the mgmt script's pattern of only removing `package.json`.\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🔧 Suggested Implementation Approach\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n1. In `Invoke-SdkRegeneration.ps1` (provisioning), instead of deleting `tspconfig.yaml`:\n   - Read the file as YAML/JSON\n   - Remove the `linter` key (or just the `$extends` within it that pulls in the problematic package)\n   - Write the modified content back to the file\n2. Remove the manual option extraction / forwarding workaround\n3. Verify the approach matches what `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/eng/scripts/Invoke-SdkRegeneration.ps1` does for `tspconfig.yaml`\n\n**Files to modify:**\n- `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-provisioning/eng/scripts/Invoke-SdkRegeneration.ps1` (line ~175)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🔗 Related Issues\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- [#56738 – Provisioning Generator tracking issue](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/56738)\n- [#56747 – AppConfiguration migration tracker](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/56747)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e","item_number":"57104"},"name":"add_comment"}}}
